subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 20
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which animal is known for having a very long neck and eating leaves from tall trees?
questions.explanation: Giraffes have incredibly long necks that help them reach food high up in trees that other animals cannot reach.
questions.hint: It has brown spots and is very tall.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their physical traits.

questions.q: What is the capital city of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital of France, world-famous for its art, culture, and the Eiffel Tower.
questions.hint: It is often called the 'City of Light'.

questions.q: Which organ in the human body do we use to think?
questions.explanation: The brain is the command center for the entire body, enabling us to think, feel, and move.
questions.hint: It is protected inside your skull.
questions.objective: Understand the function of major human body organs.

questions.q: What is the name of the force that pulls things toward the center of the Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the fundamental force that keeps us on the ground and causes things to fall.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit?
questions.explanation: Brushing your teeth twice a day helps keep them clean and healthy by removing food particles and plaque.
questions.hint: You should do this every morning and night.

questions.q: Which invention allows us to talk to someone on the other side of the world?
questions.explanation: The telephone was invented to allow instant voice communication across long distances.
questions.hint: We use them every day to connect with others.

questions.q: What is the largest continent in the world?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent by both land area and population.
questions.hint: It is located to the east of Europe.

questions.q: Which gas do plants release for humans to breathe?
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis, plants release oxygen into the atmosphere, which is vital for human life.
questions.hint: It is the gas we inhale.

questions.q: What is the boiling point of water in degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

questions.q: Which community helper is responsible for keeping the peace and enforcing laws?
questions.explanation: Police officers are trained to maintain public order and safety within a community.
questions.hint: They often drive cars with sirens.
